data.narrative:
CX2 Industries is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by Nathan Mintz and Porter Smith that has raised $31M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 peers—2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms across the same sector and stage, including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, and NODA AI.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy baseline, applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A-to-subsequent-stage progression, yielding $62M. Peer-median analysis across the 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables—valued between $125M and $500M—produces a median of $225M. The peer set exhibits material dispersion, with Stark Defence at $500M and NODA AI at $125M, reflecting variance in technology maturity, market traction, or funding momentum.

Triangulation of both methods supports a valuation range of $53M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $144M. The material gap between stage-progression ($62M) and peer-median ($225M) reflects tension between capital-efficiency mechanics and market comps. Key uncertainties include the degree to which CX2's technology differentiation, customer pipeline, and regulatory positioning align with the higher-valued peers versus the lower band. Further diligence on revenue trajectory, defense contract pipeline, and technical IP defensibility would be required to narrow this range.
